name: The Thornmark Combine
desc: A semi-autonomous trade combine whose board of directors funds sixty percent of the regional defence budget. They run three mana-refineries, a rail hub, and a licensed mercenary oversight bureau. Business is their religion — and you are affecting their margins.

responses.notice: Thornmark's Contract Review Board has flagged the Iron Company in their quarterly risk assessment. You are rated 'elevated, monitoring required'.
responses.concern: Thornmark has increased border checkpoint frequency on the fort access road. They are photographing your personnel.
responses.alarm: Thornmark's legal division has filed a formal objection with the Continental Accord Licensing Authority. Your Class B clearance is under review.
responses.hostile: Thornmark has embargoed all commercial dealings with Iron Company-affiliated accounts. Mana-credit transfers from their territory are blocked.
responses.war: Thornmark's board has authorised a full suppression contract against the Iron Company. They have already retained a competitor.

name: The Veldrath Republic
desc: A militarised republic whose standing forces number forty thousand, all augment-rated at Class A or above. Their defence minister has never lost a military engagement. He considers independent military contractors a constitutional insult.

responses.notice: Veldrath's Defence Intelligence Office has entered the Iron Company into their active monitoring database. You are Category 3. There are only four categories.
responses.concern: Veldrath's border units now carry orders to log Iron Company mana-signatures. They are not yet orders to engage.
responses.alarm: Veldrath's Defence Minister addressed a press briefing. You were the subject. The framing was not favourable.
responses.hostile: Veldrath has deployed a quiet-operations team with Iron Company personnel on the target list. They have not arrived yet.
responses.war: Veldrath has formally designated the Iron Company an unlicensed armed entity under the Defence Compact. Mobilisation orders are active.

name: The Pale Conclave
desc: A distributed ecclesiastical authority spanning seven city-states, each with its own mana-grid node consecrated to their doctrine. They maintain the most thorough arcane-monitoring network on the continent and hold licences that pre-date the Accord by three centuries. They object to your arcane activities on grounds that are simultaneously theological and regulatory.

responses.notice: The Pale Conclave's Arcane Oversight Division has published a doctrinal brief on independent mana-use. It cites the Iron Company specifically.
responses.concern: Conclave Auditors have been observed near the fort with resonance equipment. They are logging your mana-output levels.
responses.alarm: The Conclave has issued a Provisional Licence Suspension against your arcane operations in three affiliated jurisdictions.
responses.hostile: The Conclave has invoked Covenant Article 7. Iron Company personnel are barred from Conclave-licensed mana-infrastructure in any member state.
responses.war: The Conclave has issued a full Revocation Writ. Their Enforcement Division is mobilising.

name: The Iron League
desc: A coalition of six city-states sharing a unified mana-grid, a joint intelligence bureau, and a Combined Accord Military signed into treaty seventy years ago. No single member is a serious military power alone — together they have outlasted three republics and one empire. They are methodical and they do not forget.

responses.notice: The Iron League's Joint Intelligence Bureau has opened an operational file on the Iron Company. The file is currently labelled 'preliminary'.
responses.concern: The Iron League has passed an internal resolution expressing 'coordinated concern' about unlicensed military expansion. You are named in the annexe.
responses.alarm: The Iron League has formally requested that three regional authorities restrict Iron Company operational territory. Two have complied.
responses.hostile: The Iron League has activated the Mutual Suppression Compact. Member states are legally bound to deny Iron Company personnel transit and mana-credit access.
responses.war: The Iron League has invoked the Combined Military Compact. Six integrated forces are operating under unified command.

name: The Ashwood Accord
desc: The largest political entity on the continent by territorial coverage, mana-grid capacity, and standing military. The Accord Chancellor has not personally authorised action against anything smaller than a republic in thirty years. The fact that your company is now in their threat register is not a good sign.

responses.notice: An Accord Regulatory Form has arrived requiring the Iron Company to disclose personnel count, mana-output ratings, and territorial footprint. This is not routine.
responses.concern: Accord Strategic Analysis has classified the Iron Company as a 'Tier 2 Independent Military Entity'. This classification comes with passive surveillance.
responses.alarm: The Accord Parliament has formally debated the Iron Company question. No consensus was reached. That means they are still deciding how seriously to take you.
responses.hostile: The Accord has repositioned a full combined-arms division to 'regional stability operations' near your territory. They are very visible. They are not engaging. Yet.
responses.war: The Chancellor has personally signed the Suppression Mandate. The full weight of Accord military and intelligence capability is now directed at the Iron Company.
